mcq.question: What is a MAC address?
mcq.correct: A unique identifier built into a network interface card
wrong: An address used to identify a website
A device used to connect to a network

mcq.question: What is an IP address?
mcq.correct: An address used to identify a device on a network
wrong: A unique code for a website only

mcq.question: What is the main difference between a MAC address and an IP address?
mcq.correct: MAC identifies a device locally, IP identifies it on a network
wrong: IP is permanent and MAC changes
MAC is used on the internet only

mcq.question: How long is a MAC address?
What format is a MAC address written in?
What does the first part of a MAC address represent?
What does the second part of a MAC address represent?
What is IPv4?
mcq.correct: An IP system using 32 bits written in four numbers

mcq.question: Which of the following is a valid IPv4 address?
Which of the following is a valid IPv4 address?
What is IPv6?
mcq.correct: An IP system using 128 bits written in hexadecimal

mcq.question: Why was IPv6 introduced?
mcq.correct: There are not enough IPv4 addresses

mcq.question: Which of the following is a valid IPv6 address?
Which of the following is a valid IPv6 address?
What is a dynamic IP address?
mcq.correct: An address that changes when a device connects
wrong: An address used only by servers

mcq.question: What is a static IP address?
mcq.correct: An address that stays the same
wrong: An address used only on Wi-Fi
